item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a bit of a long article and I kind of skimmed it at the end, but the idea of Dr. Wondertainment passive-aggressively rubbing the information breach they caused with a board game is hilarious.

Also, I think you should remove the references to The Hateful Star since the original article was deleted and replaced with something that doesn't really match the card's description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
May also just smoke a lot of weed.

Haven't even finished yet and it's already a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have lots of questions for this SCP and I am aware of the no-canon rule.

Does the game contain memetic infohazard? Can memetic infohazard be one of the possible cards?

Does the game's version of SCP-096 have the same hazard as it's counterpart or as scp-2521?

+ More questions
- Thanks in advanced

Maksur Mode: Same as Keter Mode, only players start with a gunshot wound to a random part of their body.
Apollyon Mode: Two random, hostile supreme divine beings appear on the map after the fourth turn. Players may start the game blind.

Does the damage wear off after the game?

Secondary players who are killed or die in-game will appear outside of the distortion shortly after their death, unharmed. Primary players will remain within the anomaly for the duration of the game.

Do what happens to Primary Players?

Also how does the Foundation know that the game doesn't kill the participants and then create duplicate that have memories of playing the game?

Thaumiel Mode: Entities have reduced damage and players are not “killed”, only “knocked out”. Certain win conditions are removed. Blood and gore are no longer visible, and nudity is no longer possible, as all players have an unremovable skintight bodysuit beneath their lowest layer of clothes.

Wouldn't a baggy bodysuit be better for that purpose? Also is there any SCP that causes nudity?

Would Neutralized Mode have that bodysuit and antiviolence feature too?

Neutralized Mode: All entities and artifacts are replaced with plush versions of themselves. No entity does any damage to anything else. Lullaby music plays across the board. The game mode lacks a win condition, and just ends after all of the players have fallen asleep.

What happens when someone with Fatal Insomnia plays this mode?

item.title: God I wish this was real
item.content:encoded:
I would pay my life for this game
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this scp and I was explaining why but accidentally refreshed it. Just a quick question, are both people on a team "in"the game or is one of them a representative thats using the actual board while the other is in the game?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It will take me ages to read all the referenced skips

Also, there are 2 links to the same logs at the end of scprocedures and at the end of the entry, is it intended?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DAMN! THIS IS GOOD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone please make this an actual game. I would play it so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Articles like this are what make me so sad about the legal troubles which prevent there from ever being any sort of big-budget adaptation of the SCP Foundation. I would love to have this as an actual card game! +1 to you, my good person!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is cripplingly clever. This may be my favorite by the author; it is a grand-slam of an idea that is executed with slam-dunk detail and thought. Compositionally, it has an amazing not-too-forced justification for the inclusion of a lot of the site and the success of other articles. Most importantly to me, this is finally the marriage of the author's shrewdness for what will work with a equally formidable display of intelligence and creative wit.

Nitpicks
– hide block

I think the last logs (the game dialogue) is good only for being humored by the personnel's frustrations. That and the time it can take a game to end reminds me a lot of Monopoly, and is relatable. Other than that though, it seems superfluous, as it just repeats the game summary and isn't particularly exciting to get through.

One more nitpick:

“Observers will see the space appear to fall away, as if the viewpoint of observers outside of the area of effect has become a bird’s eye view of a space much larger than the affected area.”

This is a convoluted sentence that takes a few reads to understand, and I’m not sure I get it still. I think that it would make what this is trying to say much clearer if “the viewpoint of observers outside of the area of effect” is removed, as it is already established that we are talking about observers.

My favorite part was the rules. The interactive capability is icing on the top.
I would play this game all the time and wish it existed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if I should upvote, but I did.

There are several problems here as a story. It have problems with pace, it is too long for no reason on a narrative standpoint, it is tiring and without any climax to it.

But whatever, I just want this game! I want this game badly! This is not an SCP, this is an unholy idea that can be turned into an actual game able to kill all my spare time for years to come if ever released!

Please, we need to make this happen!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think there's something wrong here. I'm throwing money at the screen but nothing is happening.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How to complete "MR. THRESHER"'s Victory Conditions

1. Activate "Consolidation".

item.content:encoded: The link to Scp-3301 test logs is missing from its main page.

item.content:encoded: Damn I would help with that. I have a vive pro and would [data expunged] to play this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am a man of seven talents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: how did you find this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“Any player who is capable of killing another player only by saying hurtful things to them becomes MR. LAMENT, and wins the game.

Finally. A legacy worth living for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd play this. A lot. Like really.

Amazing article. Upvoted.

item.title: Re: I want to play this so badly
item.content:encoded: I was thinking of starting a project for people to help with to convert this SCP into a video game/VR game

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good job. Really makes me want to play it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure I'm not the only one who would love to see this made into a video game. The great thing is that virtually everything one needs to build a real game out of this is already in this SCP, though I do hope whoever decides to make it adds additional elements not documented here (such as even more cards and such).

If there's an indie game developer out there, you know what to do. Make this game real and I will throw my money at you.

item.title: Type of font letter
item.content:encoded: Hi, I would like to know if anyone of you know the font for making the cards for a translation I plan to do (i messaged to kaktus but he didn't messaged back yet)

item.title: Love It.
item.content:encoded:
Always love me some game based SCP's.

Makes me want to see a big "fate of the world" Stakes round.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddamn.

This is clearly a love letter to the entire site, and really works well as a direct refutation to the "No Crosslinks" attitude of Series II. Sometimes it feels a bit silly, but this bit is, oddly, my favorite:

If at any point you stop playing for more than a few days, we'll know that you're done testing it and we'll go ahead and put it on the shelves! If you don't think it's ready for the shelves, then just keep on testing it ;)

"Yeah, we're fucking with you. Whatcha gonna do about it?"

My only quibble is that the sheer number of SCPs referenced here, while enjoyable to read, doesn't really make sense in-universe in the context of a Level 2 classified article. It reveals the existence of SCP-001 and numerous other highly classified objects, all presumably to the person with the low-level clearance necessary to read this article. Leans too heavily on the 4th wall.

Other than that? The sheer amount of stuff here makes it almost impossible not to like this. So creative and inventive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking of it that way, this really would be the one thing you'd throw popular characters at, if you want it to be really fun, isn't it?

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Dr A Pancake, this post is essentially contentless, and given how many comments you've made that are similarly low-content, you're getting spammy here. Please refrain from making similar posts from now on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OH okay

item.title: Re: My pick for favorite Scip is so fucking fluctuate-y I swear to got
item.content:encoded:
Also, a bit of a suggestion: Add a list of the playable GOIs and possibly special bonuses for them. I'm thinking:

Foundation: Needs to capture less anomalies than others to get Mr. Containment.

GOC: Grounded teammate starts with a pistol (it's a pretty bad pistol).

CotBG: extra bonus from cards related to the CotBG, dependent on the card.

Fiftist: Same bonus as CotBG, but with fiftist cards.

GAW: Starts with one bonus "Silly" card. (-J, 1370 as a blue card, etc.)

Factory: Once per game, can give an opponent a black card during their turn.

Chaos Insurg.: Starts with a free red card.

UIU: Starts with 2 2/1 US Army companions.

Give other ideas if these are too OP.

item.title: My pick for favorite Scip is so fucking fluctuate-y I swear to got
item.content:encoded: This will forever be the SCP I want the most in real life. SCP + Jumanji + Card Wars + Wondertainment being memebois = Perfect Christmas Gift.

item.title: Re: Well, I got cucked once again
item.content:encoded:
Well, I read they are actually working on this game. And i wanted to make an article about a foundation card game too.

I guess I will also do it. How bad can it possibly be.

item.title: Re: Well, I got cucked once again
item.content:encoded:
You do know that this is just an article (i.e. a story) about a fictional card game based on the Foundation; it is not a real product in any sense of the word (unless kaktus or some other person actually bothered to make it).

Besides, who says that there must be only one type of card game based on the Foundation?

item.title: Well, I got cucked once again
item.content:encoded:
I had a cardgame idea. But I guess I cannot pursue it anymore, since this is a thing.

Even then, this is a great project. I will not downvote it or upvote. I'm just bummed that my idea can no longer be a thing.

item.title: Re: Wondergamement
item.content:encoded: one of my favorites. good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jumanji meets SCP
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a fan of board and card games, I have to say, I absolutely love this. I would play the hell out of this game.

(And actual computer game based on this would get all the love from me.)

item.title: Re: New Pictures
item.content:encoded:
Damn, they look real rad now. I'm not translating it any time soon, but when I do, I'll be sure to ask for the templates ;)

Edit: Actually, on second thought, I think I prefer the old version more. The current designs are cool, but I the old elegant designs fit Wondertainment more. I don't think Wondertainment would use stock images for their cards in-universe. But hey, just my opinion.

item.title: New Pictures
item.content:encoded:
I thought the old pictures were dogshit so I made new ones.

For all you translators who were asking about images on this one, hit me up. I've got templates for all the card colors, as well as instructions on how to set them up. Ez.

Hope you like em.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a moment I thought it's "Escape from Site 19" ad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Escape from Site 19 is your friend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely adore the Dr. Spanko card.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd really enjoy playing it for sure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We had SCP-087-B. We had Containment Breach. Now we need SCP-3301.

item.title: I love the subtext
item.content:encoded:
The anomalous stuff is well done, but nothing here is particularly unique. The references are fun and low-key creepy. The fact that the people who made this know enough about "planet of hands" to joke about it ominous.

What really endears this one to me is the tremendous passive aggression it represents. This is a huge blow against SCP, and they either can't or won't recognize it because you can't prove DrW's motives. They are forcing SCP employees to play their game, and it's clear from the last addendum that they are getting into the game and having fun. Not only that, the winner gets cash prizes, and it's my impression that getting money from SCPs is just about as declasse as it can get for the Foundation. Our heroes have no choice but to play a cool game and receive fabulous cash prizes, violating all their values, in an environment that rubs espionage in their face. The consequences for failure are not only a massive containment breach, but, assuming all version give gold, damage to the world economy. Dr Wondertainment has compromised the Foundation on several levels without dropping their smile.

Sorry for the long comment, but I see a lot of folks dismissing this based on the phenomenon itself and wanted to elucidate exactly what I thought made this so delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ayyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn’t a game for children, so kids who play it are really cool.

what's up, you cool babies

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I accomplished pretty much exactly what I wanted with this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think a narrative is going to fit in this scip. Not all scips require a narrative to make it work. In this case, what makes it successful is that it targets something that the community likes, a board game based on the Foundation.

It's your opinion though, and I totally respect that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So long, so little pay off
I would suggest spending more time creating a narrative and less focus on lists of rules and specific cards, there's a lot of fat there that could be trimmed.
Unfortunately it's a downvote from me

item.title: Re: Wondergamement
item.content:encoded: its an incredible article took me some years to read all of this.

item.content:encoded:

Also, love this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice SCP. I really enjoy the idea :D

item.title: Re: changes
item.content:encoded: That part really brought the article together for me. I liked the knowing ';)'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Talking of things McElroy- is that a MBMBAM reference I spy in the rules?

item.content:encoded: I've also added a collab log so go check it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. Are you going to add more cards? I want to see more cards. +1 either way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can PM the author about it if you want. But the author will very likely make the final call.

item.title: Re: changes
item.content:encoded: Thirding it.

item.title: changes
item.content:encoded:
<djkaktus> I'm well aware of what they said.
<djkaktus> I've read the comments.
<Voct> it doesn't completely fit tonally with Wondertainment,
<Voct> Okay.
<djkaktus> I just
<djkaktus> I mean it doesn't fit completely no, and I know some people might not jive with it
(…)
<Voct> I bet I can think of a better reason for the Foundation to have to play the game
<Voct> better than "Wondertainment is threatening them"
<Voct> hm
<djkaktus> If you can come up with a better reason, I'll change it
<djkaktus> Like, the point is that wondertainment knows the foundation won't just play this game unless forced.
<djkaktus> Unless there's a better reason
<Voct> a regretful note saying "we're sorry you didn't like the game, well, maybe the general public will like it better!"
<djkaktus> …huh
<djkaktus> Actually
<djkaktus> Huh

  • djkaktus considers this

<Voct> we went to all this trouble to make it for you
<Voct> and we'd hate for it to go to waste
<djkaktus> Yessssssss
<djkaktus> Ok
<djkaktus> I can get on board with this
<djkaktus> Let me write this up
(…)
<djkaktus> Hmmmmmmmmmmm
<djkaktus> I going to think about this, and make that change in the morning after I get off.
(…)
<Voct> wait
<Voct> EVEN BETTER
<Voct> it's been given to the foundation for PLAYTESTING
<Voct> "once you're done playtesting it, we'll present it to the general public"
<Voct> and if you leave it unplayed for too long
<Voct> well
<Voct> you must be done playtesting!
<Voct> ready to go to the general public!
<djkaktus> Hahahaha
<djkaktus> That is actually better
<djkaktus> I'll see what I can come up with.
<djkaktus> That's very good.
